name: Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo: Circular de Medina de Pomar
name_original: Trespaderne desde Medina de Pomar — circular por la Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo
description: Cycle 25.3 miles (40.7 km) on this moderate touring route from Medina de Pomar to Trespaderne via the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Medina De Pomar traverse a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills and varied topography, contrasting with the sharp outline of the Sierra de Tesla mountain range. The region features impressive river valleys, such as those carved by the Nela River, and sections of the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway, an old railway line converted for cycling. These geographical elements provide a range of scenic backdrops and varying gradients for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Medina De Pomar The most popular touring cycling route is Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo: Circular de Medina de Pomar, a 25.3 miles (40.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 1 minute to complete. This route utilizes a section of the historic Santander-Mediterranean Greenway. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Trespaderne – Oña Historic Town Center loop from Medina de Pomar, a difficult 35.2 miles (56.6 km) path. This route offers a challenging excursion through varied terrain, passing by the historic town center of Oña. Local touring cyclists also love the Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey – Puentedey loop from Medina de Pomar, a 26.8 miles (43.1 km) trail leading through river valleys and past the Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey, often completed in about 3 hours 38 minutes. Touring cycling around Medina De Pomar is defined by varied topography, rolling hills, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to challenging excursions. The routes in Medina De Pomar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Medina De Pomar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Medina de Pomar?
A: There are over 40 touring cycling routes around Medina de Pomar, offering a diverse range of options for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Medina de Pomar?
A: Yes, Medina de Pomar offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030211573/via-verde-santander-mediterraneo-circular-de-medina-de-pomar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo: Circular de Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that utilizes a section of the historic Santander-Mediterranean Greenway, known for its accessible terrain.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trespaderne – Oña Historic Town Center loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt; offer difficult terrain and longer distances. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40342184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentedey – Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore while touring cycling near Medina de Pomar?
A: The region boasts impressive natural features. You can cycle through river valleys, past the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purón River Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3970163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tartalés de los Montes Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The Nela River has also carved an impressive natural tunnel under Puentedey, which is a highlight on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40342347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey – Puentedey loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt;. The Ojo Guareña Natural Monument is also a significant natural landmark in the area.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, Medina de Pomar itself is a historic city center with medieval streets, the Alcázar de los Velasco, and the Convent of Santa Clara. Some routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trespaderne – Oña Historic Town Center loop from Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt;, will take you past the historic town center of Oña, offering cultural enrichment during your ride.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Many routes around Medina de Pomar are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030211573/via-verde-santander-mediterraneo-circular-de-medina-de-pomar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo: Circular de Medina de Pomar&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a convenient circular journey.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Medina de Pomar?
A: The region's varied topography and natural beauty are enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with mild temperatures and less extreme weather. Summer can be warm, while winter may bring cooler temperatures, especially in higher elevations.

Q: Are the touring cycling routes around Medina de Pomar suitable for families?
A: Yes, the area offers a range of routes, including easier, well-paved sections suitable for families. The Santander-Mediterranean Greenway, for instance, provides a relatively flat and safe environment for family cycling. Always check the route's difficulty and terrain before heading out with children.

Q: Are there dedicated greenways or cycling paths in Medina de Pomar?
A: Medina de Pomar is a key point on the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway, an old railway line converted into a cycling path. This greenway offers a dedicated and scenic route for touring cyclists, connecting to other areas and providing a smooth riding experience.

Q: Is parking available near the touring cycling routes in Medina de Pomar?
A: Medina de Pomar, as a town, typically offers various parking options, including public car parks, which can serve as convenient starting points for many routes. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling routes in Medina de Pomar?
A: While many outdoor areas in Spain are dog-friendly, specific rules can vary. Greenways and less technical routes are generally more accommodating for dogs on a leash. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you carry waste bags. Check local signage for any restrictions.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Medina de Pomar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from rolling hills to impressive river valleys, and the blend of natural beauty with historical points of interest. The variety of routes catering to different fitness levels is also frequently highlighted.
tips: It is a fortified palace from the second half of the 14th century. It is the headquarters of the Historical Museum of the Merindades.

The Museum of the Constables of Castilla is located inside the Monastery of Santa Clara de Medina de Pomar. A visit is highly recommended if you want to enjoy great works of Medina heritage, the history of the Constables and learn about the life of the Poor Clares who still live in the monastery. Entrance costs 3.5 euros.

Trespaderne has several places of interest, among them: the Romanesque bridge over the Nela river near the confluence with the Ebro river (from the 12th century), the railway station (deactivated), the old laundry, the parish church of San Vicente Martyr.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends in Medina de Pomar. You can typically find parking within the town, which serves as a convenient starting point for exploring the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally accessible, specific rules for dogs may vary depending on local regulations in Medina de Pomar and Trespaderne, especially in urban areas or protected natural zones. Always keep your dog on a leash and clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring, summer, and autumn. Spring and autumn provide milder temperatures and beautiful landscapes, while summer is ideal for longer daylight hours. Consider local weather forecasts, especially for potential heat in summer or rain in other seasons.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route along the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway or through the towns of Medina de Pomar and Trespaderne.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway section of this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. While the Greenway generally follows old railway lines with gentle gradients, sections of this circular route can be unpaved and challenging to ride. You'll encounter compacted dirt tracks, secondary roads, and compacted gravel surfaces, with some areas possibly having very coarse or loose gravel. A gravel or touring bike is recommended.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a 'Moderate bike ride' requiring 'good fitness'. While parts of the Greenway are generally easy, the presence of unpaved and potentially difficult sections, along with an elevation gain of around 290 meters, means it's not entirely flat and requires some cycling experience.

faqs.question: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through areas rich in history. In Medina de Pomar, you can visit the Alcázar of the Constables and the Santa Clara Convent Museum. The town of Trespaderne also offers points of interest, including a 12th-century Romanesque bridge over the Nela River.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes significant portions of the Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo (Burgos - Túnel de La Engaña) and also intersects with sections of the Camino de Las Merindades and the Ruta de los Monteros del Rey.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Both Medina de Pomar and Trespaderne are settlements with amenities. Medina de Pomar, being a larger historic city, offers various options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. Trespaderne also has local establishments where you can find refreshments.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect fantastic and stunning views. The landscapes are diverse, featuring rugged terrain, vibrant green vegetation, and cultivated fields that turn golden with crops. The area around Trespaderne and Medina de Pomar is particularly pleasant, with the Nela River, pine forests, oak groves, and cereal crop fields.

faqs.question: What type of bicycle is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, which includes unpaved and potentially coarse gravel sections, a gravel bike or a sturdy touring bicycle is highly recommended for comfort and performance on this route.
